evidence_summary: Establish the discovery of a massive gold-bearing quartz vein in the Arizona hills to trigger the inciting incident. The scene must transition from the physical exhaustion of prospecting to the high-stakes planning of a claim defense and resource acquisition.

In the Arizona hills, 1865, John Carter and James Powell discover a massive gold-bearing quartz vein. They agree on a plan: Powell will head back to civilization for machinery while Carter stays to guard the claim.
From hard labor/prospecting to sudden euphoria and ambitious planning.
Characters engaged in heavy physical labor/prospecting.
Characters standing over the claim, finalizing a division of labor.
Discovery of gold leads to an agreement on a strategic plan.
